finding: `node atm.mjs git commit` can time out at the agent/tool layer while its spawned pre-commit hook continues running heavyweight validators, so the operator sees a timeout even though the commit later succeeds.
expectedBehavior: Governed commit should expose a durable transaction id, phase log, resumable status command, or timeout-aware progress output so agents can distinguish "still validating" from "safe to retry/repair".
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by extending `git commit-status` with `evidence.branchCommitQueueStatus`: current branch queue lock path, lock record, owner PID liveness, age, current HEAD, head-moved signal, and wait-vs-inspect guidance. Regression coverage in `tests/cli/git-commit-timeout-and-status.test.ts` now simulates a live queue owner and requires `status: busy`, `ownerAlive: true`, and wait guidance.
followUp: Keep the live branch queue diagnostic in `git commit-status`; future commit transaction work can add richer phase logs, but operators must never have to guess whether a timed-out wrapper is still committing.
